title: Conjecture 3
statement_text: There is a function $f$ such that, for any fixed tree $T$ with maximum degree $\Delta_T$, every $f(\Delta_T)$-edge-connected graph with minimum degree at least $f(|E(T)|)$ and number of edges divisible by $|E(T)|$ has a $T$-decomposition.
context_text: Posed in [4] as a strengthened, minimum-degree version of the Barát–Thomassen conjecture, replacing a single large edge-connectivity constant by a function of the maximum degree of $T$. The case $\Delta_T = 2$ (paths) was proved in [4] (Theorem 4 of the paper under review). This paper investigates whether the conjecture holds for trees of higher maximum degree.
paper_contribution: The paper disproves Conjecture 3 for trees of maximum degree three by constructing a counterexample based on complete binary trees $T_k$ of arbitrary depth $k$, showing that no function $f$ can make the conjecture hold in that case.
notes: PDF source; LaTeX reads cleanly. The authors of reference [4] are not named in the body text.
